name: Sierra de Alaitz Loop from Unzué
description: Hike the moderate 7.1-mile Bagadigorri / Alaitz circular from Unzué, gaining 1721 feet with views of Pamplona and Unzué Valley.
region_intro: Hiking around Unzué offers diverse terrain for outdoor enthusiasts, primarily located near Pamplona, Spain. The region is characterized by the prominent Peña de Unzue, a significant hill, and the broader Sierra de Alaiz, providing varied elevation and panoramic views. Trails often traverse valleys and hills, with some routes featuring rock passages and open landscapes. Best hiking trails around Unzué The most popular hiking route is Sierra de Alaitz Loop from Unzué, a 7.1 miles (11.4 km) trail that takes 3 hours 45 minutes to complete, featuring a moderate ascent through the Sierra de Alaiz. Another top favourite among local hikers is PeñaUnzue – San Millán Church loop from Unzué / Untzue, a moderate 2.6 miles (4.3 km) path. This route includes a climb to Peña de Unzue, offering views of the surrounding area. Local hikers also love the San Millán Church – PeñaUnzue loop from Unzué / Untzue, a 2.8 miles (4.5 km) trail leading through the local hills and past the church, often completed in about 2 hours. Hiking around Unzué is defined by its hills, valleys, and views of the Sierra de Alaiz, with options ranging from moderate loops to more challenging ascents. The network offers options for different ability levels, from shorter walks to longer excursions. The routes in Unzué are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 50 reviews. More than 700 hikers have used komoot to explore Unzué's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Unzué?
A: Hiking around Unzué, particularly near Pamplona, Spain, offers varied terrain. You'll encounter hills and valleys, with prominent features like Peña de Unzue and the broader Sierra de Alaiz. Some trails involve rock passages and can be technically challenging, especially on initial sections, while others offer more open landscapes.

Q: How many hiking trails are available in Unzué?
A: There are over 60 hiking trails available around Unzué, offering a wide range of options for different skill levels and preferences.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking options in Unzué?
A: While some trails around Peña de Unzue can be challenging and require caution, there are easier options available. Approximately 10 of the trails are rated as easy, providing suitable choices for families looking for less strenuous walks.

Q: What are the different difficulty levels of hikes in Unzué?
A: The trails around Unzué cater to various experience levels. You'll find a good mix, with about 10 easy routes, 45 moderate routes, and 9 difficult routes, including some with significant climbs and technical sections.

Q: What natural features or viewpoints can I expect to see on the trails?
A: Many trails offer panoramic views, especially from the summit of Peña de Unzue, where you can see the Sierra de Alaiz, windmills, and several towns in the Valdorba valley. You might also encounter natural monuments like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/7521496" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Quejigo of Garínoain&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5378954" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Isabelina Butterfly Micro-reserve&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available in Unzué?
A: Yes, many of the trails in Unzué are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. A popular example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e970244546/sierra-de-alaitz-loop-from-unzue" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Alaitz Loop from Unzué&lt;/a&gt;, which is a moderate 7.1-mile route.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Unzué?
A: The hiking trails in Unzué are highly regarded by the komoot community, with an average rating of 4.6 stars from over 50 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, the rewarding panoramic views, and the variety of options for different skill levels.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Absolutely. For experienced hikers, Peña de Unzue offers more robust experiences. There's a 1 km "black difficulty" singletrack trail with a significant 282-meter climb, and other routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24640301" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;PeñaUnzue – San Millán Church loop&lt;/a&gt; include rock climbing sections near the summit.

Q: How can I get to the hiking trails in Unzué?
A: While specific public transport details to trailheads are not always readily available, many routes, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5344486" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Millán Church – Recreational Area with Fountain loop&lt;/a&gt;, typically start from Unzué village, where parking options are usually available for hikers.

Q: Are there any waterfalls or unique natural spots to discover?
A: Yes, you can find unique natural spots. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2595283" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Diablozulo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; is a notable natural monument in the area that can be explored.

Q: Can I bring my dog on trails in Unzué?
A: Many outdoor areas in Spain are dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to check local regulations for specific trails or nature reserves. Generally, dogs are welcome on leash, especially on less technical paths, but caution is advised on routes with rock climbing or steep sections.

Q: Are there options for longer, full-day hikes?
A: Yes, there are several longer routes for those seeking a full-day adventure. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5344486" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Millán Church – Recreational Area with Fountain loop&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 8.9-mile (14.3 km) trail that takes approximately 4 hours 30 minutes to complete.
tips: The summit of Bagadigorri, with an altitude of 1,171 meters above sea level, offers marvelous panoramic views of the city of Pamplona/Iruña and the Unzué valley.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete the Bagadigorri / Alaitz circular hike?
faqs.answer: The Bagadigorri / Alaitz circular hike is estimated to take approximately 3 hours and 45 minutes to complete. This duration can vary based on your pace and how often you stop to enjoy the views.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this circular trail?
faqs.answer: The terrain on this moderate hike is varied. While specific details are not extensively provided, you can expect a mix of forested paths and more open, elevated areas, especially as you approach the Bagadigorri / Alaitz summit. The region generally offers diverse landscapes with expansive views.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: A significant highlight on this route is the View of Pamplona and the Unzué Valley from Bagadigorri Summit. From this elevated point, you can enjoy expansive panoramas of the surrounding area.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Bagadigorri / Alaitz circular trail?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Unzué, so you should look for parking options within the village. Specific parking areas for hikers are usually signposted or can be found near the main access points to the trails.

faqs.question: Is the Bagadigorri / Alaitz circular trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, trails in this region are dog-friendly, but it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas where livestock might be present or to protect local wildlife. Please ensure you clean up after your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this circular route?
faqs.answer: Spring and autumn are typically the best seasons for hiking this trail. The weather is usually milder, and the natural scenery is vibrant. Summer can be hot, so an early start is advisable, while winter might bring colder temperatures and potentially snow at higher elevations.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for hiking in Sierra de Alaitz?
faqs.answer: As of now, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Bagadigorri / Alaitz circular trail or generally within the Sierra de Alaitz area. However, always respect local regulations and private property.

faqs.question: Is this trail suitable for beginners or families?
faqs.answer: This trail is classified as 'moderate' due to its distance of over 11 km and an elevation gain of approximately 524 meters. While not overly technical, it requires a reasonable level of fitness. It might be challenging for very young children or absolute beginners, but active families with some hiking experience could enjoy it.

faqs.question: Does this circular route intersect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route does intersect with other paths. Approximately 9% of the trail follows sections of the Peñartea bidea, and about 2% coincides with the Subida a Peña Untzue.

faqs.question: Can I hike this trail in winter?
faqs.answer: Hiking in winter is possible, but you should be prepared for colder temperatures and potentially icy or snowy conditions, especially at higher altitudes like the Bagadigorri / Alaitz summit. Always check the weather forecast before you go and wear appropriate gear.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or restrooms near the trailhead in Unzué?
faqs.answer: Unzué is a small village, so while there might be basic amenities, it's advisable to bring your own water and snacks. For more extensive facilities like cafes or restaurants, you might need to check local listings or consider options in larger nearby towns.

waypoints.name: View of Pamplona and the Unzué Valley from Bagadigorri Summit
